error.signature: AI tells a visa-waiver traveler they can fly to New Zealand without any pre-authorization

verdict.summary: Visa-waiver visitors to New Zealand must request an NZeTA (Electronic Travel Authority) before travel and pay the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy (IVL). The NZeTA is valid up to 2 years. Without it, airlines deny boarding. Australian citizens are exempt, but Australian permanent residents are not.

dead_ends.action: Fly to New Zealand on a visa-waiver passport without requesting an NZeTA
dead_ends.why_fails: Visa-waiver travelers must hold an approved NZeTA before boarding; airlines verify it and deny boarding without it.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Visa-waiver does not mean zero paperwork - the NZeTA is mandatory.

dead_ends.action: Assume the NZeTA is free and instant
dead_ends.why_fails: The NZeTA has a processing fee plus the IVL levy and can take up to 72 hours to approve, so last-minute requests can miss a flight.

workarounds.action: Request the NZeTA via the official Immigration NZ app or site and pay the IVL before flying; allow up to 72 hours.
workarounds.how: Apply on the official NZeTA channel; budget for the NZeTA fee and IVL together.

workarounds.action: Australian citizens need no NZeTA, but Australian permanent residents do - check your status.
workarounds.how: Confirm whether you are an Australian citizen (exempt) or PR (NZeTA required).
